title: False BLKSEQ warning for verilog v1995
body:
Verilator mistakenly thinks an explicit sensitivity list means a sequential logic process, which in the case below is not so.
This bug is a somewhat recent regression.

Can you please attach an example that shows the issue or missing feature?  (Must be openly licensed, completely self-contained so can directly run what you provide. Ideally use test_regress format, see https://veripool.org/guide/latest/contributing.html?highlight=test_regress#reporting-bugs)

**xtime.v**
```verilog
`default_nettype none
`timescale 1ns/1ps

module xtime(
  lhs,
  o
);
  input  wire [7:0] lhs;
  output reg [7:0]  o;
  wire [7:0] shifted;
  always @(shifted or lhs)
  begin
    if (lhs[7]) o = shifted ^ 8'h1b;
    else o = shifted;
  end
  assign shifted = lhs << 1;
endmodule
```

What output from that test indicates it is wrong, and what is the correct or expected output?  (Or, please make test self-checking if possible.)

```
%Warning-BLKSEQ: xtime.v:13:19: Blocking assignment '=' in sequential logic process
                              : ... note: In instance 'xtime'
                              : ... Suggest using delayed assignment '<='
   13 |     if (lhs[7]) o = shifted ^ 8'h1b;
      |                   ^
                 ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/BLKSEQ?v=5.041
                 ... Use "/* verilator lint_off BLKSEQ */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
%Warning-BLKSEQ: xtime.v:14:12: Blocking assignment '=' in sequential logic process
                              : ... note: In instance 'xtime'
                              : ... Suggest using delayed assignment '<='
   14 |     else o = shifted;
      |            ^
```


What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?
`verilator_bin.exe --lint-only --assert --quiet-stats --top-module xtime --timing --default-language 1364-1995 -Wall -Wno-fatal xtime.v`

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?  Did you try it with other simulators?
`Verilator 5.041 devel rev v5.040-201-g21dbdbf69`

What OS and distribution are you using?
Windows, from oss-cad-suite (Linux was the same)

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?
Unfortunately, no.
